summary: Review #2 after sourcery-ai[bot] feedback. Three improvements were made: (1) restrictedSecurityContext() helper moved from reconcile_deployment.go to a dedicated security_context.go file, (2) tests now use findContainerByName() instead of index-based access, (3) duplicated assertion logic extracted into expectRestrictedSecurityContext() helper in security_context_test.go. All four builder functions (buildDBSyncJob, buildBootstrapJob, fernetRotationCronJob, credentialRotationCronJob) continue to set SecurityContext on every container via the shared helper. All tests pass. go vet clean.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist for all four builder functions (db-sync, bootstrap, fernet, credential)
Tests cover both init containers and main containers (fernet, credential CronJobs)
Tests use name-based container lookup instead of brittle index access
Shared expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper validates all four PSS fields
TestRestrictedSecurityContext unit test exists for the helper function itself
All existing tests continue to pass without modification

code_quality_checklist.item: restrictedSecurityContext() is in a dedicated neutral file (security_context.go)
No duplicated assertion logic across test files
SPDX headers present on new files
Unused ptr import cleanly removed from reconcile_deployment.go

security_checklist.item: SeccompProfile RuntimeDefault set on all containers

architecture_checklist.item: Cross-cutting helper in dedicated file, not domain-specific file
Single source of truth for restricted SecurityContext definition
Test helpers co-located with production helper in security_context_test.go

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated SecurityContext assertion blocks across test files
No scope creep beyond requested changes

fail_fast_checklist.item: findContainerByName returns nil for missing container, caught by expectRestrictedSecurityContext nil check

defensive_checklist.item: expectRestrictedSecurityContext checks container is not nil before accessing fields
expectRestrictedSecurityContext checks SecurityContext is not nil before dereferencing pointers

detected_patterns.name: Cross-cutting security helpers in dedicated files with shared test assertions
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new cross-cutting security or configuration helper that is used by multiple reconciler files, with corresponding test assertions that would otherwise be duplicated
detected_patterns.description: Cross-cutting helpers (e.g., restrictedSecurityContext) are placed in a dedicated file named after their concern (security_context.go), not in a domain-specific reconciler file. The corresponding test file (security_context_test.go) co-locates the unit test for the helper alongside shared test assertion functions (expectRestrictedSecurityContext) and utility functions (findContainerByName) that consumer tests import. This prevents assertion drift across test files and keeps the helper's location neutral.

examples.code:
func expectRestrictedSecurityContext(g Gomega, container *corev1.Container) {
	g.Expect(container).NotTo(BeNil(), "container must exist")
	sc := container.SecurityContext
	g.Expect(sc).NotTo(BeNil(), "SecurityContext must be set on container %q", container.Name)
	g.Expect(sc.AllowPrivilegeEscalation).NotTo(BeNil())
	g.Expect(*sc.AllowPrivilegeEscalation).To(BeFalse())
	g.Expect(sc.RunAsNonRoot).NotTo(BeNil())
	g.Expect(*sc.RunAsNonRoot).To(BeTrue())
	g.Expect(sc.ReadOnlyRootFilesystem).NotTo(BeNil())
	g.Expect(*sc.ReadOnlyRootFilesystem).To(BeTrue())
	g.Expect(sc.SeccompProfile).NotTo(BeNil())
	g.Expect(sc.SeccompProfile.Type).To(Equal(corev1.SeccompProfileTypeRuntimeDefault))
}
